FFmpeg — license & corresponding-source notice
===============================================

This application bundles unmodified FFmpeg binaries (ffmpeg.exe and
ffprobe.exe) for audio decoding/encoding/trimming. These binaries are NOT
part of the Audio Transcriber source code; the application invokes them as
a separate process (subprocess), never linking FFmpeg into its own code.

Build bundled here
------------------
  ffmpeg version 8.1.1-essentials_build-www.gyan.dev
  configuration includes: --enable-gpl --enable-version3 (among others)

License
-------
  Because this FFmpeg build is configured with --enable-gpl --enable-version3,
  it is distributed under the GNU General Public License, version 3 (GPL v3)
  or (at your option) any later version.

  The full, authoritative GPL v3 license text is available at:
      https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt

Corresponding source (written offer)
------------------------------------
  The complete corresponding source code for this FFmpeg build is available
  from:
    * https://www.gyan.dev/ffmpeg/builds/   (the exact Windows build vendored here)
    * https://git.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg.git     (upstream FFmpeg source repository)
    * https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg      (source mirror)

Relationship to Audio Transcriber
----------------------------------
  Audio Transcriber itself is licensed under the MIT License (see the
  project's top-level LICENSE file). FFmpeg is a separate work distributed
  under its own GPL v3 terms. Bundling FFmpeg as an independent executable
  and calling it via subprocess does not place the Audio Transcriber source
  under the GPL (mere aggregation / use at arm's length).

  Note: the gyan.dev distribution ships its own LICENSE/README files with
  the full text. If you re-vendor FFmpeg, prefer copying gyan.dev's original
  license files here verbatim alongside the binaries.
